author: b.hartnett
content: But this is not ideal for performance and increases the size of the BAL when the tracked precompile addresses are not needed. In eip-2929 precompiles are pre-warmed (I guess because they don't require any state trie lookup?) so it doesn't seem ideal to record them in the BAL.

author: b.hartnett
content: This would be fine in my opinion. If the balance is updated then the account access is needed but not otherwise.

author: b.hartnett
content: One potential issue with this idea is the account empty check for EIP-161 which requires the account to be loaded even for precompile addresses but since no empty accounts exist now (since the merge?) this check should no longer be required and clients can skip it as an optimization. See https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7523. Whether clients have implemented this optimisation or not would create differences in the BAL if we didn't track all precompiles accessed.
